summary: Migration 010 correctly creates channels, claude_sessions, channel_session_links, sync_cursors tables and renames goal_tasks to tasks with nullable goal_id. New repositories (ChannelRepository, ClaudeSessionRepository, SyncCursorRepository) and their tests are well-written and pass. However, session-repo.test.ts (19 tests) fails because the migration renames the sessions table to _deprecated_sessions, breaking the existing SessionRepository which still queries the sessions table. The GoalTaskRow type also has a column name mismatch (thread_id vs channel_id).

issues.description: SessionRepository queries 'SELECT * FROM sessions' but migration 010 renames sessions to _deprecated_sessions. This causes all 19 session-repo tests to fail with 'SqliteError: no such table: sessions'. The SessionRepository must either be updated to query _deprecated_sessions, or (better) the session-repo.test.ts should be adapted since SessionRepository is being replaced by ChannelRepository + ClaudeSessionRepository. At minimum, the deprecated table reference needs to match.

GoalTaskRow still has 'thread_id: string | null' but the actual tasks table (after migration 010) uses 'channel_id'. The GoalTaskRepo code accesses 'row.channel_id' (line 284 of goal-task-repo.ts), which works at runtime but doesn't match the TypeScript type. Should update GoalTaskRow.thread_id to channel_id, or use the new TaskRow type.

Migration inserts from both sessions and archived_sessions into claude_sessions without ON CONFLICT handling. If the same id exists in both tables (unlikely but possible), the second INSERT will fail with a PRIMARY KEY conflict. Consider using INSERT OR IGNORE.
